title: lavandula canariensis subsp. hierrensis
intro: Lavandula canariensis subsp. hierrensis, also known as Hierro lavender, is a subspecies of the Canarian lavender plant that is endemic to the island of La Gomera in the Canary Islands. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and inflammation, and calm digestive issues. The health benefits of Hierro lavender include promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving sleep quality, as well as its ability to support wound healing and skin health. Hierro lavender is typically used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
